summary: Patrick McKenzie announces his interview on Mixergy with Andrew Warner is now live after being rescheduled due to technical difficulties. The interview covers his experience running Bingo Card Creator while working full-time as a Japanese salaryman, his early traction strategies, and hints at an upcoming new product. He notes the interview was recorded at 2 AM due to time zone differences between Japan and the US, which affected his coherence at times.
key_points: The Mixergy interview covers McKenzie's dual life as a full-time Japanese salaryman and solo software entrepreneur
Key topics include early traction strategies, process reliability, and maximizing limited time
McKenzie teases a new product but declines to reveal details, promising a May announcement
The practical challenges of being an international bootstrapper are illustrated by recording at 2 AM due to time zones
money_quotes: Given that it was about 2 in the morning when I recorded it due to time zone differences, sometimes my ability to speak in coherent sentences leaves a bit to be desired.
key_insight: Building a software business while working full-time in Japan meant navigating not just limited hours but practical challenges like recording interviews at 2 AM due to time zone differences.
